unit.name: Beyond images: audio, video, embodied settings, 3D, smell, and future trends
unit.description: Extends the lecture beyond images and closes with forecasts about multimodal foundation models.
unit.summary: The final section broadens the lecture beyond image-text pairs to speech, audio, video, embodied environments, 3D generation, and even smell [ts=4134s][ts=4464s]. Whisper is highlighted as a large-scale speech model trained on 680,000 hours of multilingual transcribed audio using log-mel spectrogram inputs into a transformer, and Kiela notes that audio can often be treated as a vision-like representation via spectrograms [ts=4185s][ts=4204s][ts=4245s]. For video, he argues that many existing image-text recipes transfer with frame subsampling or keyframe selection, and mentions trimodal systems such as MERLOT Reserve that add audio on top of video-and-language modeling [ts=4352s][ts=4380s]. He closes with more speculative directions—simulated environments for grounded learning, text-to-3D generation, olfactory embeddings, multimodal scaling laws, retrieval augmentation, and the idea that future foundation models will integrate many modalities while still needing much better evaluation [ts=4495s][ts=4587s][ts=4665s][ts=4679s].

key_points.text: Whisper trains on 680,000 hours of multilingual multitask speech data using log-mel spectrograms and a transformer.
Audio can sometimes be reduced to a spectrogram and processed with standard vision architectures to obtain strong auditory features.
Video models often subsample frames or keyframes and then reuse familiar vision-language transformer recipes.
MERLOT Reserve is an example of a trimodal model that combines video, language, and audio.
Simulated environments may be important because humans ground language by acting in and observing the world.
Text conditioning can extend beyond images to 3D generation such as point clouds or CAD-like design.
Olfactory embeddings can be built from chemical-compound statistics and may correlate with human similarity judgments better than pure word vectors for smell-grounded concepts.
Future directions include one general multimodal foundation model, multimodal scaling laws, retrieval augmentation, and much better evaluation.

kp_catalog.name: Beyond images: audio, video, embodied learning, and new modalities
kp_catalog.description: Generalizing the multimodal recipe to speech, audio, video, interaction, 3D generation, and even olfactory grounding.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because beyond images: audio, video, embodied learning, and new modalities extends the core thread to generalizing the multimodal recipe to speech, audio, video, interaction, 3D generation, and even olfactory grounding without being required for the main pipeline.

kp_catalog.name: Evaluation and benchmark design
kp_catalog.description: The need to build tests that force multimodal reasoning and expose shortcut behavior, compositional failures, and distributional bias.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because evaluation and benchmark design formalizes how the need to build tests that force multimodal reasoning and expose shortcut behavior, compositional failures, and distributional bias, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: Unified multimodal foundation models
kp_catalog.description: The idea that one jointly pretrained model should handle unimodal and multimodal tasks rather than requiring separate systems.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because unified multimodal foundation models synthesizes the idea that one jointly pretrained model should handle unimodal and multimodal tasks rather than requiring separate systems into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
